channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Fight Escalates Over Plan to Demolish and Rebuild N.Y.C. Public Housing
item.description: The city thinks four developments in Chelsea are too run-down to be saved, and wants to rebuild them, adding mixed-income housing. Some residents are opposed.
item.dc:creator: Mihir Zaveri

media:content.media:credit: Lila Barth for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Public housing buildings on the campuses of the Fulton and Elliott-Chelsea Houses, left, have deteriorated over the years, even as the surrounding neighborhood of Chelsea has revitalized.

item.title: ¿La IA amenaza el empleo? Cada vez más economistas lo piensan
item.description: Los economistas afirman que la inteligencia artificial no ha alterado el mercado laboral, pero cada vez están más convencidos de que lo hará y de que los políticos no están listos.
item.dc:creator: Ben Casselman

item.title: She Wanted Her Hair Done for a Special Day. She Left the Salon in Tears.
item.description: A Black woman is pushing for changes in the hair industry after claiming in a lawsuit that she and her 7-year-old were turned away from an Ulta Beauty salon in Manhattan because of their hair texture.
item.dc:creator: Skylar Mitchell
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations

media:content.media:credit: Eva Woolridge for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Lauren Smith, who lives in West Harlem, said a stylist at an Ulta Beauty salon on the Upper East Side of Manhattan refused service to her and her 7-year-old daughter. They both have textured hair.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Hints for April 4, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Las influentes de talla grande que disfrutan Disney World
item.description: Cinco mujeres disfrutan de las atracciones en uno de los parques más famosos a nivel mundial sin que su peso sea un impedimento.
item.dc:creator: Madison Malone Kircher and Mary Beth Koeth
item.category: Disney, Walt, World (Lake Buena Vista, Fla)

media:content.media:description: Las Plus Size Park Hoppers visitan los parques de Disney tan seguido como sus presupuestos y agendas de trabajo les permiten, normalmente unas 6 veces al año.

item.title: Por qué el matrimonio es cada vez menos atractivo para mucha gente
item.description: La soltería ya no es vista como un fracaso en EE. UU., y vivir juntos o enfocarse en la independencia personal se ha convertido en una alternativa legítima.
item.dc:creator: Tammy LaGorce

item.title: Iran War Live Updates: U.S. Forces Search for Missing Airman After Iran Downs Jet
item.description: The F-15E was the first U.S. warplane shot down by Iran in the war. The episode presents challenges for the United States that would grow if the missing airman is taken prisoner.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: Epstein Presented Himself to Indian Tycoon as a Trump White House Insider
item.description: The convicted sex offender gave Anil Ambani information on appointments and foreign policy. Some seemed prescient, though there was no evidence he was close to the administration.
item.dc:creator: Anupreeta Das

media:content.media:description: Anil Ambani, an Indian businessman who exchanged emails with Jeffery Epstein for more than two years, in 2022 in Mumbai.

item.title: Quote of the Day: The Mystifying Drunken Episodes of Teetotalers
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Saturday, April 4, 2026.

item.title: Trump Directs Officials to Pay All D.H.S. Employees
item.description: The memorandum calls for paying employees at the Coast Guard, Federal Emergency Management Agency and Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency who have gone without pay during a record-long shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Zolan Kanno-Youngs

media:content.media:description: President Trump had hinted that he would sign an order calling for Department of Homeland Security employees to be paid.

item.title: Chart-Toppers
item.description: Royce Ferguson’s latest Saturday puzzle drops some sick beats.
item.dc:creator: Caitlin Lovinger

media:content.media:description: Don’t get rocked by this blocky grid!

item.title: ‘Iran Is Being Destroyed in Front of Our Eyes’: Tehran Is Gripped by Fear
item.description: Fifteen residents of Tehran said in telephone interviews and text messages that the capital was weathering heavy bombardment Friday.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Residents in front of buildings damaged by airstrikes in Karaj, Iran, on Friday.

item.title: Arrest of Wisconsin Mosque Leader Was Tied to Trump Antisemitism Campaign
item.description: Immigration lawyers and former federal officials say the case of Salah Sarsour echoes those against other pro-Palestinian activists.
item.dc:creator: Jazmine Ulloa and Hamed Aleaziz

item.title: Trump Contemplates Other Cabinet Changes as He Faces Political Clock
item.description: With the midterms approaching, the president may be running out of time to get new cabinet members confirmed without bipartisan support.
item.dc:creator: Zolan Kanno-Youngs, Rebecca Davis O’Brien and Julian E. Barnes

media:content.media:description: President Trump has contemplated making additional changes to his cabinet.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Friday
item.description: An American fighter jet was shot down by Iran for the first time since the start of the war, leading to an urgent search-and-rescue operation for a missing crew member.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Amit Elkayam for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A home in Ramat Gan, Israel, damaged by a projectile during an Iranian missile strike on Friday.

item.title: Avión militar de EE. UU. derribado en Irán: lo que sabemos
item.description: Un helicóptero de rescate también fue atacado y otro avión militar de la Fuerza Aérea, un A-10 Warthog, se estrelló el viernes en la región del golfo Pérsico.
item.dc:creator: Neil Vigdor
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Un avión de combate F-15E Strike Eagle como este fue derribado por Irán el viernes.

item.title: Trump Administration Celebrates Good Friday in Official Messages
item.description: President Trump and administration officials have extended more pronounced overtures to Christians in public statements and executive orders.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: On Friday, the White House posted a message from President Trump: “On Good Friday, the Son of God was nailed to the cross, crucified, and He died for all of us. It was a day of darkness, but it wasn’t the end.”

item.title: What if Iran Wins This War?
item.description: If Iran wins this war, victory “will be tremendously emboldening for a regime that has been very dangerous even at its weaker moments,” Suzanne Maloney, the director of foreign policy at the Brookings Institution, argues on “The Ezra Klein Show.”
item.dc:creator: ‘The Ezra Klein Show’

item.title: What if Trump Just Walked Away From Iran?
item.description: Iran will become “the toll collector at the gulf” if Trump preemptively walks away from the conflict in Iran, Suzanne Maloney, the director of foreign policy at the Brookings Institution, argues on “The Ezra Klein Show.”
item.dc:creator: ‘The Ezra Klein Show’

item.title: Why Iranians Believe They Have the Upper Hand
item.description: Iran believes time is on its side, and that the West isn’t ready for the economic shock of a blocked Strait of Hormuz, warns Suzanne Maloney, the director of foreign policy at the Brookings Institution.
item.dc:creator: ‘The Ezra Klein Show’

item.title: ICE Review Finds Dozens of Violations at Texas Migrant Detention Center
item.description: A review of Camp East Montana in Texas found failures in medical care, disease control and oversight, including improperly documented uses of force.
item.dc:creator: Jesus Jiménez

media:content.media:credit: Paul Ratje for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Large tents meant to house thousands of immigrants held at Camp East Montana, a detention facility at Fort Bliss in El Paso, Texas.

item.title: Our First Moon Colony Will Be Filled With Garbage
item.description: Forget futuristic glass domes filled with plants and high-tech infrastructure, Jared Isaacman, the head of NASA, says of humanity’s first space colonies. In reality, because of the cost of travel, they will most likely be scrappy bases surrounded by garbage.
item.dc:creator: Interesting Times

item.title: Trump Goes After Programs He Calls ‘Woke’ in Budget Proposal
item.description: The president’s request for 2027 reflects his preoccupation with eliminating programs that support diversity and civil rights.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater and Michael C. Bender

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Russell T. Vought, the White House budget director, testifying in front of the Senate Appropriations Committee last summer.

item.title: After 11 Years in Court, Heir Reclaims a Modigliani Looted by the Nazis
item.description: A judge ruled against a holding company controlled by David Nahmad, the billionaire art dealer, which had bought the work at auction in 1996.
item.dc:creator: Graham Bowley

media:content.media:description: “Seated Man With a Cane,” painted by Amedeo Modigliani in 1918.

item.title: Stray Bullet That Killed Baby Girl in Brooklyn Also Grazed Brother
item.description: Two men have been arrested in connection to a shooting on Wednesday that killed an infant. One was charged with murder, attempted murder and assault.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Maag

media:content.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The baby was rushed to a nearby hospital but was pronounced dead shortly after.

item.title: NASA's Artemis II Is the First Crewed Moon Mission Since 1972. Why Are We Going Back?
item.description: These lunar resources could be valuable to us on Earth and in space.
item.dc:creator: Marco Hernandez, Malika Khurana and Kenneth Chang

item.title: One of Two Americans Rescued After Iran Downs a U.S. Jet
item.description: Also, job growth rebounded in March. Here’s the latest at the end of Friday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Cullen

media:content.media:description: A U.S. Air Force F-15E fighter jet taking off from RAF Lakenheath in the United Kingdom in 2020.

item.title: Ex-US Army Pilot Recalls Battle for Survival When Shot Down in Iraq
item.description: Ronald Young Jr. was in the Army when his Apache Longbow copter went down during the U.S. invasion of Iraq.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Ronald Young Jr. boarding a transport plane near Baghdad in 2003, after escaping captivity.

item.title: Carol Mullins, Who Illuminated Dance for Decades, Dies at 86
item.description: She knew nothing about lighting when the director Robert Wilson asked her to work on his shows. She then spent over 40 years as a designer for Danspace Project.
item.dc:creator: Brian Seibert

media:content.media:credit: George Etheredge for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Carol Mullins in 2024. In 1982, she got a call from the director of Danspace Project in Manhattan, offering her a position as the organization’s resident lighting designer. It was a job that Ms. Mullins would keep until her death.

item.title: Search and Rescue Underway After Iran Downs U.S. Fighter Jet
item.description: Search and rescue efforts continued after a U.S. fighter jet was shot down over Iran. One of the two crew members was rescued, but the fate of the other was unknown.
item.dc:creator: Jamie Leventhal, Aric Toler, Haley Willis and Artemis Moshtaghian

media:content.media:description: The U.S. aircraft that was brought down over Iran has been identified as an F-15E Strike Eagle. One of its two-member crew is still missing.

item.title: Iran’s Defenses Have Been Struck, but They Can Still Fire Missiles and Drones
item.description: Iran has sent waves of missiles and drones at Israel and Gulf states in recent weeks and shot down an American fighter jet on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Kulish
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Planet Labs Pbc/Planet Labs PBC, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: This satellite photo shows damage after an Iranian attack at Al Udeid Air Base in Qatar last month. Iran struck the regional air headquarters of U.S. Central Command, damaging an early-warning radar system.

item.title: High Gas Prices Hamper Holy Week Pilgrimage in the Philippines
item.description: Worshipers have scaled back and adjusted plans for the Visita Iglesia, a Holy Week tradition in which Catholics travel to seven churches in one day for prayer, as the war in Iran has doubled gas prices in the Philippines.
item.dc:creator: Christina Kelso, Daniel Fetherston and Ezra Acayan
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Filipinos heading to the provinces for the Holy Week lining up at a bus terminal in Quezon City, Philippines, on Thursday.

item.title: Dolores Keane, Singer Known as the ‘Soul of Ireland,’ Dies at 72
item.description: She sang of homesickness and longing, and often collaborated with American musicians like John Prine and Emmylou Harris.
item.dc:creator: Clay Risen

media:content.media:description: Dolores Keane performing in 1990. She emerged in the 1970s as part of a renaissance in the traditional music of Ireland.

item.title: In the Philippines, Rising Fuel Prices Force Travelers to Stay Home During Holy Week
item.description: Surging gasoline prices in the Philippines have forced some people to cancel or scale back the Visita Iglesia, a Holy Week tradition in which Catholics travel to seven churches.
item.dc:creator: Francesca Regalado and Ezra Acayan
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Filipinos heading to the provinces for the Holy Week lining up at a bus terminal in Quezon City, Philippines, on Thursday.

item.title: ‘Under Protest,’ Raw Dairy Farm Recalls Cheddar Linked to 9 E. Coli Cases
item.description: Raw Farm, a dairy producer in California, denies that its products have caused the outbreak, which has sickened people in three states.
item.dc:creator: Alice Callahan
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

media:content.media:description: Raw Farm shredded Cheddar cheese on display at a grocery store in Los Angeles in 2023.

item.title: Six Senate Races to Watch as Democrats Grow Bullish Ahead of Midterm Elections
item.description: It’s still a tall task for the party to win back control. Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Shane Goldmacher

item.title: Forest Service Will Close Research Stations That Study Wildfire Risk
item.description: Scientists say their work on fires and climate change could be lost as the agency moves its headquarters to Utah from Washington and shuts 57 research stations.
item.dc:creator: Eric Niiler

media:content.media:credit: John Stember for The New York Times

item.title: Iran Shoots Down U.S. Fighter Jet, Setting Off American Search and Rescue
item.description: One crew member was rescued. The risky operation to find the second airman came as the United States and Israel struck infrastructure targets in Iran, prompting Iranian retaliation in the Gulf.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt, Helene Cooper and Thomas Fuller
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: What We Know About the F-15E Strike Eagle Shot Down by Iran
item.description: An F-15E Strike Eagle was lost to enemy fire and an A-10 Warthog crashed in the Persian Gulf region on Friday, officials said. A rescue helicopter was also fired upon.
item.dc:creator: Neil Vigdor
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: An F-15E Strike Eagle, the type of plane shot down in Iran.

item.title: Preschool Instructor Charged With Sexually Assaulting Children
item.description: A 43-year-old man was accused of abusing “multiple” children over an 11-year period in South Jersey, and prosecutors fear there could be more victims, given his employment.
item.dc:creator: Tracey Tully

media:content.media:credit: Jonah Markowitz for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: William E. Reynolds, the Atlantic County prosecutor, called the details of the allegations “heinous.”

item.title: NASA’s Artemis II Astronauts Took iPhones Into Space
item.description: The astronauts traveling in the Artemis II spacecraft were allowed to take smartphones with them. Sadly, they can’t connect to the internet.
item.dc:creator: Kalley Huang

item.title: Iran Is Quickly Repairing Missile Bunkers, U.S. Intelligence Says
item.description: Reports cast doubt on how close the United States is to destroying Iran’s missile capability, a key goal in the war.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes and Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: The tail section of a missile in Israel’s northern Jordan Valley on Friday. Iran has continued to strike at the country, even with its reduced arsenal and more careful use of launchers.

item.title: Primeras fotos de la Tierra de la misión Artemis II
item.description: Las imágenes tomadas desde la cápsula Orión por el comandante de la misión, Reid Wiseman, muestran distintas vistas de nuestro brillante planeta azul.
item.dc:creator: Katrina Miller

item.title: ‘The Drama’ and the Secrets That Can Unravel a Relationship
item.description: In the new film, a shocking revelation upends the lives of a couple played by Zendaya and Robert Pattinson, raising the question: Are some betrayals simply unforgivable?
item.dc:creator: The Styles Desk

item.title: President Trump Makes Contradictory Statements About Strategy for War in Iran
item.description: Since launching the war in Iran on Feb. 28, President Trump has altered his position on regime change and shifted the timeline of operations.
item.dc:creator: McKinnon de Kuyper and Zach Wasser

item.title: Two Diaries of War, From Opposing Sides of Iran’s Political Divide
item.description: One supports the regime, the other desires change. Each offers a personal account of the terrifying sights and frayed emotions of a city under attack.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: A Tehran resident taping her windows to keep them from shattering during bombings.

item.title: H.H.S. Takes a First Step Toward Restoring Vaccine Advisory Committee
item.description: A federal judge last month questioned the legitimacy of the panel and overturned its recent work. A notice suggests that it may be revived on Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s terms.
item.dc:creator: Apoorva Mandavilli

media:content.media:description: The membership and decisions of the Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices, which recommends vaccines for Americans, were halted last month by a federal judge.

item.title: Trump Seeks $152 Million to Begin to Turn Alcatraz Back Into a Prison
item.description: The plan faces significant local political opposition and the dilapidated state of the site poses considerable logistical challenges.
item.dc:creator: Soumya Karlamangla and Heather Knight

media:content.media:credit: Ian C. Bates for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Alcatraz Island, located in the San Francisco Bay near San Francisco, hosts 1.4 million visitors every year.

item.title: The F-15E Strike Eagle: What to Know
item.description: The U.S. military aircraft with a two-person crew was shot down in Iran on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: The U.S. aircraft that was brought down over Iran has been identified as an F-15E Strike Eagle. One of its two-member crew is still missing.

item.title: Flashback: Your Weekly History Quiz, April 4, 2026
item.description: Can you sort 8 historical events?
item.dc:creator: Marina Wang
